main.name: Keeton Martin
main.description: I am a software engineer and I've put together this page to showcase my work. Welcome to my home base on the internet
main.bio: I graduated from Haverford College with a B.S. in Computer Science in May 2022 and now work as a software engineer at Range in New York. I build AI systems, product infrastructure, and full-stack tools for wealth management and investment planning. Outside of work I like to play and think about basketball, fiddle with keyboards, travel, and enjoy the great outdoors.

resume.skillmessage: I build agent-first products and tools that point toward the future of software.

education.degree: Bachelor of Science in Computer Science
education.description: Graduated with a 3.7 GPA and minors in Neuroscience and Statistics. Coursework included Databases, Software Engineering, OS Concurrency, Data Structures, Algorithms, AI, Probability, and Advanced Regressions.
Focused on data science, computer science, and statistics. AP Scholar with Honors and ACT 35.

work.title: Software Engineer
work.description: Software engineer on the Investments team and tech lead for Range's flagship AI financial planning product, building broker-dealer and RIA systems for wealth management. I ship AI systems for proactive advisor outreach, day-one personalized financial plans for new customers, and a rebuilt customer-facing agent on a modern tool-calling framework. I also developed internal benchmarks for AI coding tools and agents to guide engineering tooling decisions.

work.title: Software Engineer (MTS)
work.description: Designed, built, and shipped Flow features in Automation Services using TypeScript and Java. Led a greenfield ETL project that scaled from 0 to 1.2M transactions in six months, including a UI for non-technical users to configure field mappings, SQL joins, and aggregations with guardrails against misconfiguration. Promoted to mid-level engineer in February 2024 and mentored a summer intern through successful project completion.

work.title: Software Engineer Intern
work.description: Built an OpenAPI describe utility for DataRaptors in the Salesforce core monolith, helping generate REST API interface definitions for a key Salesforce Industries tool. Completed independent enterprise software work with guidance from a Senior Architect and Project Manager.

work.title: Software Engineering Intern
work.description: Developed and published a Node.js and TypeScript Salesforce DX plugin using OCLIF, with automated Jenkins deployment to npmjs.org. Built the command-line tool independently from scratch with guidance from a Senior Engineer.
